steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare the curviness of the Embarras River (high sinuosity) and the Luni River (low sinuosity). The correct answer must directly state which river has the greater stream-to-valley length ratio.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the Luni has a higher sinuosity than the Embarras': reverses the data — the Luni has low sinuosity, the Embarras has high sinuosity. ✗
  • Choice B — 'sinuosity is defined as the stream-to-valley length ratio': defines sinuosity rather than comparing the two rivers. ✗
  • Choice C — 'a river with a sinuosity above 1.5 is considered highly sinuous': states a general definition, not a direct comparison of the Embarras and Luni. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the Embarras has a greater stream-to-valley length ratio than the Luni': directly compares the two rivers and matches the data (Embarras high sinuosity, Luni low sinuosity). ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
